q: What was the birth name of Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA)?
explanation: His name was Abdullah. Before Islam, he was called Abdul Kaaba, and the Prophet (PBUH) renamed him Abdullah.

q: Who was the father of Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA)?
explanation: Uthman bin Amir, better known as Abu Quhafa, accepted Islam on the day Makkah was conquered.

q: Which daughter of Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA) was married to the Prophet (PBUH)?
explanation: Hazrat Aisha (RA) was the daughter of Abu Bakr (RA) and became the wife of the Prophet (PBUH).

q: Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA) belonged to which branch of the Quraish?
explanation: He was from the Banu Taym clan.

q: Who was the 'Sidiq' (The Truthful)?
explanation: The Prophet (PBUH) gave him this title after he immediately testified to the truth of the Isra and Mi'raj.

q: Which Caliph led the 'Apostasy Wars' (Ridda Wars)?
explanation: After the Prophet's (PBUH) death, several tribes rebelled and followed false prophets; Abu Bakr (RA) suppressed them firmly.

q: Who was the false prophet defeated at the Battle of Yamama?
explanation: Musaylimah the Liar was killed by Wahshi bin Harb (RA) during the caliphate of Abu Bakr (RA).

q: Who suggested the compilation of the Quran into a single volume during Abu Bakr's era?
explanation: Hazrat Umar (RA) suggested it after many Huffaz (memorizers) were martyred at Yamama.

q: Who was the primary scribe tasked with compiling the first copy of the Quran?
explanation: Zaid bin Thabit (RA) was chosen for his skill as a scribe and his memorization of the Quran.

q: How long was the caliphate of Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA)?
explanation: He ruled from 632 AD to 634 AD, a short but crucial period.

q: What was the profession of Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA)?
explanation: He was a wealthy and respected cloth merchant in Makkah.

q: Who accompanied the Prophet (PBUH) in the Cave of Thawr during Hijrah?
explanation: Abu Bakr (RA) was the 'Companion of the Cave'.

q: At what age did Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA) pass away?
explanation: He died at the same age as the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

q: Who did Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA) nominate as his successor?
explanation: After consulting the senior companions, he nominated Hazrat Umar (RA) to ensure continuity.

q: Hazrat Umar (RA) belonged to which Quraish clan?
explanation: He was from the Banu Adi clan.

q: What was the title of Hazrat Umar (RA)?
explanation: Al-Farooq means 'The one who distinguishes between truth and falsehood'.

q: In which year of Prophethood did Hazrat Umar (RA) accept Islam?
explanation: His conversion followed Hazrat Hamza's (RA) and significantly strengthened the Muslims.

q: Who introduced the 'Hijri' calendar?
explanation: Hazrat Umar (RA) established the calendar, starting from the year of Hijrah.

q: Which Caliph conquered Jerusalem (Bait-ul-Maqdas) and received the keys of the city in person?
explanation: Hazrat Umar (RA) traveled to Jerusalem to sign the peace treaty with Patriarch Sophronius.

q: What was the name of the administrative consultative body established by Hazrat Umar (RA)?
explanation: The Majlis-e-Shura consisted of companions who advised on state matters.

q: Who established the first judicial system with independent judges (Qadis)?
explanation: Umar (RA) separated the judiciary from the executive for the first time.

q: Which great empire was completely defeated during Hazrat Umar's caliphate?
explanation: The conquest of Persia was completed after the Battle of Nahavand.

q: How long was the caliphate of Hazrat Umar (RA)?
explanation: He ruled for approximately 10 years (634 AD to 644 AD).

q: How was Hazrat Umar (RA) martyred?
explanation: A Persian slave named Abu Lu'lu'a Firuz stabbed him while he was leading the prayer.

q: Where is Hazrat Umar (RA) buried?
options: Beside the Prophet (PBUH) and Abu Bakr (RA)
explanation: He was granted permission by Hazrat Aisha (RA) to be buried in her chamber.

q: Hazrat Usman (RA) belonged to which Quraish clan?
explanation: He was a member of the powerful Banu Umayya clan.

q: What was the title of Hazrat Usman (RA)?
explanation: Zun-Nurain means 'The possessor of two lights', as he married two of the Prophet's daughters.

q: Which daughters of the Prophet (PBUH) did Hazrat Usman (RA) marry?
explanation: He married Ruqayyah first, and after her death, he married Umm Kulthum.

q: Why is Hazrat Usman (RA) called 'Al-Ghani' (The Wealthy/Generous)?
options: He donated massive wealth to Islam
explanation: He was extremely wealthy but spent it generously for the Muslim cause, such as buying the Well of Ruma.

q: Which Caliph ordered the standardization of the Quran's recitation and the distribution of official copies?
explanation: To avoid confusion in different regions, he standardized the Quraishi dialect version.

q: Which Islamic naval fleet was first created during Hazrat Usman's era?
explanation: Under the leadership of Muawiyah (RA), the first Muslim navy defeated the Byzantines at sea.

q: How long was the caliphate of Hazrat Usman (RA)?
explanation: He served as Caliph for 12 years (644 AD to 656 AD), the longest among the Rashidun.

q: How was Hazrat Usman (RA) martyred?
options: Assassinated while reciting the Quran in his house
explanation: Rebels besieged his house and killed him while he was fasting and reading the Quran.

q: Where is Hazrat Usman (RA) buried?
explanation: He is buried in the Jannat al-Baqi cemetery in Madinah.

q: Hazrat Ali (RA) was the son of who?
explanation: Abu Talib was the uncle and guardian of the Prophet (PBUH).

q: What was the relation of Hazrat Ali (RA) to the Prophet (PBUH)?
explanation: He was the Prophet's (PBUH) cousin and married his youngest daughter, Hazrat Fatimah (RA).

q: What was the title given to Hazrat Ali (RA) for his bravery?
explanation: The Prophet (PBUH) called him Asadullah for his courage in battles like Khaybar.

q: Who were the sons of Hazrat Ali (RA) and Hazrat Fatimah (RA)?
explanation: Hasan and Hussain are the grandsons of the Prophet (PBUH) and leaders of the youth of Paradise.

q: Where did Hazrat Ali (RA) move the capital of the Islamic state?
explanation: He shifted the capital from Madinah to Kufa for strategic reasons.

q: Which battle was fought between Hazrat Ali (RA) and Hazrat Aisha (RA)?
explanation: The Battle of Jamal (36 AH) was the first major internal conflict.

q: Which battle was fought between Hazrat Ali (RA) and Amir Muawiyah (RA)?
explanation: The Battle of Siffin took place in 37 AH near the Euphrates river.

q: Who were the 'Kharijites' (Khawarij)?
options: A group that broke away from Ali's army
explanation: They separated after the arbitration at Siffin and later rebelled against Ali (RA).

q: How was Hazrat Ali (RA) martyred?
explanation: The Kharijite Ibn Muljam struck him with a poisoned sword while he was going for Fajr prayer in Kufa.

q: Where is the tomb of Hazrat Ali (RA) generally believed to be?
explanation: The Imam Ali Mosque in Najaf is his shrine.

q: How long was the caliphate of Hazrat Ali (RA)?
explanation: He ruled from 656 AD to 661 AD.

q: Which Caliph is known as 'Bab-ul-Ilm' (The City/Gate of Knowledge)?
explanation: The Prophet (PBUH) said 'I am the city of knowledge and Ali is its gate'.

q: Who was the first woman to accept Islam?
explanation: Hazrat Khadija (RA), the Prophet's wife, was the absolute first person to believe.

q: Which Caliph established the first state-run Bait-ul-Mal (Treasury)?
explanation: Hazrat Umar (RA) formalized the distribution of wealth and state finances.

q: Which companion was the son of 'The Martyr of the Trench' and became a famous general?
explanation: Usama bin Zaid was the son of Zaid bin Haritha and led an army at 18.

q: Who were the 'Ashara Mubashshara'?
options: Ten companions promised Paradise in one setting
explanation: These ten companions, including the four caliphs, were given the glad tidings of Jannah during their lives.

q: Which Caliph ordered the digging of administrative canals for agriculture?
explanation: Hazrat Umar (RA) initiated many irrigation projects across newly conquered lands.

q: Who was the 'Secretary of the Rashidun Caliphate'?
explanation: Zaid bin Thabit (RA) was the primary scribe for both the Prophet (PBUH) and the Caliphs.

q: Which Caliph is known for his extreme modesty (Haya)?
explanation: The Prophet (PBUH) said even the angels felt shy before Hazrat Usman (RA).

q: Who defended the Prophet (PBUH) at Uhud when most had scattered?
options: Hazrat Ali and Musab bin Umair
explanation: Hazrat Talha (RA) sustained over 70 wounds protecting the Prophet (PBUH).

q: What was the 'Saqifah Bani Sa'idah'?
options: The place where Abu Bakr was elected as Caliph
explanation: The Ansar gathered there to discuss leadership, leading to the selection of Abu Bakr (RA).

q: Who was the mother of Hazrat Ali (RA)?
explanation: She was one of the early women converts and very close to the Prophet (PBUH).

q: Which Caliph introduced the title 'Amir-ul-Momineen' for himself?
explanation: Abu Bakr (RA) was called 'Khalifat-ur-Rasool', while Umar (RA) introduced 'Amir-ul-Momineen'.

q: Who was the governor of Syria during the caliphates of Umar and Usman?
explanation: Muawiyah (RA) served as governor for 20 years before becoming Caliph himself.

q: Which Caliph was known as 'The Conqueror of Persia' indirectly?
explanation: The bulk of the Persian empire fell during Hazrat Umar's (RA) reign.

q: Which companion was given the title 'Ameen-ul-Ummah' (The Trustworthy of the Nation)?
explanation: Prophet (PBUH) called Hazrat Abu Ubaydah (RA) the trustworthy of this Ummah.

q: What was the name of the sword of Hazrat Ali (RA)?
explanation: It was a famous double-tipped sword given to him by the Prophet (PBUH).

q: Who was the first person to lead a congregational 'Tarawih' prayer in Ramadan?
explanation: Hazrat Umar (RA) organized the scattered prayers into one congregation behind one Imam.

q: Which companion was the 'Interpreter of the Quran' (Tarjuman al-Quran)?
explanation: Ibn Abbas (RA), the Prophet's cousin, was known for his vast knowledge of Tafsir.

q: Who was the 'Sword of Allah' (Saif-ullah)?
explanation: The Prophet (PBUH) gave this title to Khalid bin Walid (RA) during the Battle of Mu'tah.

q: Which Caliph initiated the expansion of Masjid al-Haram (Kaaba) for the first time?
explanation: Hazrat Umar (RA) bought surrounding houses to widen the space for pilgrims.

q: Who was the first governor of Egypt?
explanation: Amr bin al-Aas (RA) conquered Egypt and became its first Muslim administrator.

q: Which Caliph established a regular military pension and salary system?
explanation: Hazrat Umar (RA) set up the 'Diwan' to record and pay soldiers and their families.

q: Who was the 'Hawari' (The Disciple) of the Prophet (PBUH)?
explanation: Prophet (PBUH) said, 'Every Prophet has a disciple, and my disciple is Zubair'.

q: Which scholar among the companions was known as 'Hibr-ul-Ummah' (The Doctor/Sage of the Nation)?
explanation: Ibn Abbas (RA) was called this due to his deep sea of knowledge.

q: Who was the leader of the Muslims at the Battle of Yarmouk (primary command)?
explanation: Khalid bin Walid (RA) led the unified Muslim forces to a decisive victory.

q: At which place did Hazrat Ali (RA) defeat the Kharijites?
explanation: The Kharijite movement was nearly eliminated at Nahrawan.

q: Which companion was a famous expert in the 'Science of the Faraih' (Inheritance Laws)?
explanation: Prophet (PBUH) said Zaid is the most knowledgeable in inheritance.

q: What was the name of the pact in which Muslims and Meccans agreed to peace?
explanation: This treaty took place in 6 AH.

q: Who was the father of Hazrat Usman (RA)?
explanation: He was from the Umayyad branch of the Quraish.

q: How many years did the Rashidun Caliphate last in total?
explanation: According to the Hadith, 'The Caliphate after me will be for thirty years', which encompasses the four caliphs.

q: Which Caliph created the title 'Al-Siddiq' status for the whole community's belief?
explanation: His unwavering faith became a standard for the Ummah.

q: Who was the wife of the Prophet (PBUH) and daughter of Hazrat Umar (RA)?
explanation: Hazrat Hafsah (RA) was married to the Prophet (PBUH) after her first husband was martyred.

q: Who was the commander who conquered Cyprus during Hazrat Usman's era?
explanation: The navy led by Muawiyah (RA) captured Cyprus in 28 AH.

q: Which companion was the hero of the conquest of Iraq?
explanation: Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas (RA) won the Battle of Qadisiyah.

q: Who was the 'Muazzin' during the caliphates of Abu Bakr and Umar?
explanation: Hazrat Bilal (RA) continued to call the Adhan, though he mostly retired to Syria after the Prophet's death.
